Okay, welcome to section 14. This is going to be the perfect for you if close. And it is purposely titled that because we don't want right for you. Like my whole rant on satisfaction in the last module, what does right even mean? We really have to qualify and quantify it and give it a clear image. So the perfect for you if close is about showing your prospect that your goal is not to sell them but to help them come to the best decision. In other words, coaching them in or coaching them out. It's all about genuinely being on their side, taking the sting out of decision anxiety and creating a very real criteria of what makes the perfect buyer poised to receive the benefits of your product or service. And that's why I like to use the frame. If you had to place real money on the success of your clients and in reality you are what characteristics attributes, experiences, abilities, achievements would you look for to feel like you're making a safe bet. So the top references for this would be the 67 golden questions visualizations, particularly the million dollar bet where I do invite you to go into the frame you just spoke about. So if you haven't already done it or need a refresher, just go back to that million dollar bet exercise and have it ready for writing this. Now when you present the criteria attributes of the perfect buyer, what you're really doing is you're creating this archetype or this persona for your reader to self identify with. So how do we coach decisiveness through self identification? Well here's a loose theme plate I like to use and this does work really well as an email as well. So the general format is as follows. So the headline will be you're ready to achieve x outcome if and version two that would be x number of ways to know you're ready to achieve y outcome. The first point I'd like to bring up is the person who's just getting started and wants to do this right. Following by the person already struggling who wants to fix it once and for all. And something that exclusively empowers them to use your product or service and a general desirable trait or characteristic something like willing to put in the work but being a little more specific about it and then backing it up. You know for example you're already investing four hours per week actively trying to achieve that outcome. A big fear or concern that your USP addresses. Further they are aware and excited about the big outcome. They're aware and excited about the quickest and most valuable win. So this is the QVW that we're going to speak about more in the next lesson but the short version is it's really the quickest and most valuable thing they can produce using your product and they are ready to take the minimum viable commitment. So that might mean something like take 14 days to put x component or x module or x feature into practice to try to achieve y. And then finally you're going to do what I call a criteria close make it very specific. You know if they could raise their hand and say yes to at least six of those nine points or four of those seven whatever you end up using then you're creating a very clear pass score for them to say yes this is worth me giving a try to achieve that outcome. Now we're going to break down a few examples in the next video. If you do need inspiration I invite you to check those out to see how they really play out in real time. And when you are ready take a moment take a few moments grab a fresh cup of coffee and write your perfect for you if close and I'll see you in the next video.
